name: Cripple Clarence Lofton
abstract: Clarence Lofton (March 28, 1887, 1896 or 1897 – January 9, 1957), credited as Cripple Clarence Lofton, was an American boogie-woogie pianist and singer born in Tennessee.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: Solo Art was -- at that time he had recorded four black pianists, Cripple Clarence Lofton, Jimmy Yancey, Pete Johnson, Albert Ammons... and now he wanted to record George Zach, and this is the story of how I happened -- no, Dan decided to give me a date. No, that's -- I'm almost mixed up. He gave me

about_quotes.speaker: John "Knocky" Parker
about_quotes.evidence: We had, uh, the, uh, Railroad Express, that old fellow who created Walking Base before Yancey. I'll think of the name in just a minute, I know. Railroad Express. Lorenzo. Cripple Clarence Lofton. Yeah, sure. Cripple Clarence, of course.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Lovett
about_quotes.evidence: I don't think I ever actually played with him, but I was there one night when he came in and sat down and played.
